title: Static Files Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of the use of express.static() middleware to automatically serve image, CSS, and JS assets from a public directory.

questions.question: Which and/or what is the primarily role of 'Static Files' in a web application?
options: Assets like images, CSS styles, and client-side JavaScript that are sent directly to the user's browser without being changed by the server.
To hide the JavaScript code from the console.

questions.question: Which built-in Express middleware is used to serve a directory of static files to the browser?
If you have an image at 'public/img/logo.png' and use 'app.use(express.static('public'))', what URL will accessing that image in the browser?
Which and/or what was the primarily benefit of using 'express.static'?
options: It handles requests for images and styles efficiently without needing to write custom code for each file.

questions.question: Does following a solid 'Static Files' strategy help build truly Fullstack applications by providing a place for all your styles and images to live?
options: Yes, as it is a core feature of any modern server-side framework.
